name: Roasted Red Pepper Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the peppers directly on the oven racks in a preheated 500°F (260°C) oven for about 20 minutes, turning occasionally so all sides are evenly charred. Cover the bottom of the oven with aluminum foil to catch any drips if a pepper splits open. The skins should be blackened and blistered when done. When the peppers are cooled enough to handle, transfer them to a covered bowl. Cooling in their own steam makes peeling easy.
2. Remove the stems, thick inner membranes, and seeds from the peppers by slicing into thin strips and then cutting those strips lengthwise. You should have at least 2 cups. Set aside.
3. In a large skillet, sauté the onion in olive oil for a few minutes. Add garlic and continue sautéing until translucent. Add tomatoes, basil, and black pepper, stirring to combine with onions. Stir in the red pepper strips and cook until heated through.
4. Puree about one-third of the sauce using a blender or food processor, then return it to the pan. Finish by adding parsley. Serve hot over pasta.
